Japanese: ６月２２日　午後１２時　４分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: June 22, 12:04 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、ホントなの‥‥？ おばさまのコト‥‥。
English: ...Is it really true... about my aunt...?

Japanese: のどかさん１人で、霧崎先生を 殺害するのはムリだ。
English: I'm afraid so, Maya. There is no way Ini could've killed Dr. Grey by herself.

Japanese: 条件に当てはまるのは‥‥ キミ子さんしかいない。
English: And under the circumstances, there's no one else other than your aunt, Morgan.

Japanese: そんな‥‥ヒドいよ。 どうして、おばさまが‥‥？
English: That... That's terrible... Why? Why would my aunt...?

Japanese: ‥‥予想どおりの しめっぽさね、成歩堂　龍一。
English: Everything is going just as I predicted, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: あっ！　かるま検事！
English: Ack! Ms. von Karma!

Japanese: どういうコトですかッ！ なるほどくんにフクシューなんて！
English: Why are you doing this!? Why are you trying to take revenge on Nick!?

Japanese: あなたのお父さんのコトは、 なるほどくんのせいじゃ‥‥
English: Nick had nothing to do with what happened to your dad.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなコトより。
English: Don't you have something better to be worrying about?

Japanese: あなたは自分のシンパイを することね‥‥おじょうさん。
English: Say, your own situation?

Japanese: う。 ‥‥同い年なのに‥‥。
English: Ugnn... ...Looking down on me, even though we're the same age...

Japanese: ‥‥マンゾクか？ ぼくを苦しめることができて。
English: Are you done yet? You've cause me enough pain and suffering.

Japanese: まだよ。
English: Not yet.

Japanese: 私の目的は、あなたを倒して、 それを全世界に知らしめるコト。
English: My goal is to defeat you, and let the whole world know of your defeat.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなことをしたって、 きみのお父さんは帰ってこないよ。
English: ...But even if you do that, it won't bring your father back...

Japanese: フッ‥‥まあ、いいわ。
English: Hmph. ...Whatever.

Japanese: とりあえず、この勝負に ケリをつけましょう。
English: In the meantime, let's bring this match to its conclusion.

Japanese: そのとき、 すべてがアキラカになるわ！
English: And then we'll know who the real winner is.

Japanese: 同日　午後１２時１０分 地方裁判所　第２法廷
English: June 22, 12:10 PM District Court Courtroom No. 2

Japanese: では、審理を再開しましょう。 ‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Court will now reconvene. Mr. Wright.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: 葉中　のどかの動機を 立証してくれるのでしたな？
English: Can you prove Ms. Ini Miney had a motive?

Japanese: ‥‥おまかせください。
English: Yes, I believe I can.

Japanese: （‥‥葉中　のどかと霧崎医師を むすんでいるポイントは１つ）
English: (Ini Miney and Dr. Grey have only one point of connection.)

Japanese: （１年前の、交通事故。 そこに何かがあるはずだ‥‥）
English: (And that is the car accident one year ago. The motive I'm looking for must be there...)

Japanese: 証人。‥‥１年前の交通事故 について、証言してください。
English: Ms. Miney. Please testify to this court about your car accident last year.

Japanese: あれえ？　私の動機を 聞かせてくれるんですよねえ？
English: Huh? I thought, like, you wanted to, like, ask about my, like, motive.

Japanese: そんなの、ヒトコトでスパッと 言えないんですかあ？
English: I, like, don't see the point in, like, bringing up the past.

Japanese: 証人。おねがいします。
English: Ms. Miney, if you please.

Japanese: ‥‥ハイハイ。 ムダだと思いますけどお。
English: ...OK, OK. Like, I totally don't think it'll do any good, but...

Japanese: ～１年前の交通事故～
English: -- Last Year's Accident --

Japanese: あれは‥‥、 １年前の５月のことでしたあ。
English: That was... like, last year, in May.

Japanese: お姉さんの病院は当時、 タイヘンなコトになっていて‥‥。
English: Like, something really bad had happened at, like, my sister's clinic around then...

Japanese: その夜も、運転中のお姉さん、 つかれきっていましたあ。
English: And like, the night of the accident, my sis was totally tired while she was driving.

Japanese: 私もつい、 助手席で眠っちゃって‥‥
English: I was, like, totally pooped too, so I, like, fell asleep in the passenger's seat...

Japanese: スゴい衝撃で目がさめたら、 まわりはもう、火の海でぇ。
English: I, like, woke up 'cause of a jolt, and, like, it was a sea of flames around me.

Japanese: ドアを開けて逃げるのが やっとでしたあ。
English: I, like, opened the door and, like, got away.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。その話なら 聞いたことがあります。
English: Hmm... I think I've heard of this incident.

Japanese: １年前のワイドショーで、 毎日さわがれていましたからね。
English: It was all the talk on the tabloid shows, day after day, last year.

Japanese: たしか、霧崎医師が‥‥お姉さんに 睡眠薬を盛ったという‥‥
English: Yes, and there was talk about Dr. Grey... drugging your sister...?

Japanese: くひッ！
English: Gwuugh!

Japanese: ただの“ウワサ”よ。 ‥‥コンキョなど、ない。
English: Those were merely "rumors". ...Totally baseless gossip.

Japanese: ううう‥‥、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Hmm, yes... Mr. Wright, you may question the witness.

Japanese: ‥‥“霧崎先生が睡眠薬を 盛ったかどうか？”‥‥
English: Right now, it's impossible to prove

Japanese: ‥‥今さら、そんなことを 立証するのはムリよ。
English: that Dr. Grey did in fact drug Ms. Miney's sister.

Japanese: ええ。‥‥別の方向から 攻めてみます。
English: Yeah... Which means I'll have to work this from another angle.

Japanese: ～１年前の交通事故～
English: -- Last Year's Accident --

Japanese: あれは‥‥、 １年前の５月のことでしたあ。
English: That was... like, last year, in May.

Japanese: お姉さんの病院は当時、 タイヘンなコトになっていて‥‥。
English: Like, something really bad had happened at, like, my sister's clinic around then...

Japanese: その夜も、運転中のお姉さん、 つかれきっていましたあ。
English: And like, the night of the accident, my sis was totally tired while she was driving.

Japanese: 私もつい、 助手席で眠っちゃって‥‥
English: I was, like, totally pooped too, so I, like, fell asleep in the passenger's seat...

Japanese: スゴい衝撃で目がさめたら、 まわりはもう、火の海でぇ。
English: I, like, woke up 'cause of a jolt, and, like, it was a sea of flames around me.

Japanese: ドアを開けて逃げるのが やっとでしたあ。
English: I, like, opened the door and, like, got away.

Japanese: （この証言の中に、何か ヒミツが眠っているはず‥‥）
English: (There's got to be some sort of secret sleeping in this testimony...)

Japanese: そのとおりよ。 決定的なムジュンを探しだすの！
English: Yes, so let's find that one crushing contradiction and end this!

Japanese: 私、免許を持ってないから、運転を かわってあげられなかったんです。
English: I, like, didn't have my license, so, like, I couldn't take over driving for her.

Japanese: たしか、お姉さんの病院で 医療ミスがあったのも‥‥
English: Didn't some sort of medical malpractice happen at your sister's hospital then...?

Japanese: そうですねえ。 それも５月でしたよお。
English: Yeah, like, that was in May too.

Japanese: ５月の２日に、患者さんが 亡くなる事件があってえ、
English: Like, the thing with all the patients dying was, like, May 2nd,

Japanese: 私たちが事故にあったのは ２４日でしたあ。
English: and like, our accident was, like, on the 24th.

Japanese: たてつづけに、事故‥‥。 グーゼンなのでしょうか？
English: Two accidents back to back... Do you assume it's just a coincidence?

Japanese: つかれてましたからねえ。 ‥‥お姉さん。
English: My sis was, like, totally tired, so that's why, duh.

Japanese: “１４人の患者が亡くなった 医療ミス”‥‥でしたね？
English: That was the malpractice incident where 14 patients died, correct?

Japanese: そうですぅ。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: そのミスの原因は なんだったんですか？
English: What was the cause of the mistake?

Japanese: おクスリをまちがえた、 って聞いてますけどお。
English: Like, I heard it was 'cause the medicines, like, got mixed up.

Japanese: それは、お姉さん‥‥ 葉中　未実さんがまちがえた？
English: Was that the fault of your sister, Mimi Miney?

Japanese: ちがいますよお。 霧崎が、お姉さんのせいに‥‥
English: Like, no way. Dr. Grey, like, wanted to blame it on my sis...

Japanese: その事故は、今回の事件には 関係ないはず。
English: That incident has nothing to do with our murder here.

Japanese: ‥‥それに、警察の捜査によって、 葉中　未実のミスは立証された。
English: Besides, the police report has already documented that it was entirely Mimi Miney's fault.

Japanese: （つまり、ジジツは どうであれ‥‥）
English: (Which means as far as the "real" facts are concerned...)

Japanese: （記録の上では、葉中　未実の ミスだった、ということか‥‥）
English: (Mimi Miney is the one who made the mistake...)

Japanese: お姉さんじゃないのに‥‥。
English: But, like, it really wasn't my sis...

Japanese: “つかれきっていた”‥‥？
English: She was "totally tired"? You mean, exhausted?

Japanese: 連日、警察の取り調べや取材が ありましたからねえ。
English: Like, she had to talk to the police, and, like, was being investigated, like, every day.

Japanese: ‥‥ホント、ヒドい 状況だったんですよお。
English: It was, like, a totally terrible situation.

Japanese: じゃあ、交通事故を起こしても フシギではなかったわけですね。
English: It's no wonder, then, that the car accident happened, huh?

Japanese: （お姉さんのことを聞いても しかたない‥‥）
English: (There's no point in asking her about her sister.)

Japanese: （もっと、のどかさんの情報を 集めるんだ‥‥！）
English: (I should be trying to find out more information about Ini!)

Japanese: いいですかあ、つづけますよお。
English: So, like, can I go on?

Japanese: どうして、かわって あげなかったんですか？
English: So then, why didn't you ask to switch places?

Japanese: え‥‥。 な、何をですかあ？
English: Huh? Like, what are you talking about?

Japanese: 運転ですよ、ウンテン。
English: I meant switch drivers.

Japanese: お姉さんがつかれていたのなら、 あなたがすればよかったのに。
English: If your sister was so tired, then you should have switched with her.

Japanese: それもそうですねえ。
English: True, true.

Japanese: あ、でも、私‥‥ 免許、持ってませんからあ。
English: Ah, but like... I don't, like, have a driver's license...

Japanese: 証人は免許を持っていなかった。 ‥‥初耳ね、私も。
English: This is news to me. I was not aware you didn't have your license, Ms. Miney.

Japanese: そうですね。‥‥証人、 今の証言をつけ加えてください。
English: Hmm... Ms. Miney, please amend your testimony.

Japanese: はあい、わかりましたあ。 とにかく‥‥
English: Like, OK. Anyway...

Japanese: お姉さん‥‥未実さんは、 そんなにつかれていたんですか？
English: Your sister... Mimi Miney, was she that exhausted?

Japanese: 連日、警察の取り調べや取材が ありましたからねえ。
English: Like, she had to talk to the police, and, like, was being investigated, like, every day.

Japanese: ‥‥ホント、ヒドい 状況だったんですよお。
English: It was, like, a totally terrible situation.

Japanese: あなたが免許を持っていれば、 かわってあげられましたのにねえ。
English: Ah, if only you had your license and were able to drive for your sister...

Japanese: え、ええ。ザンネンですう‥‥。
English: Y-Yeah... That was, like, too bad...

Japanese: ‥‥では、証人。 証言をつづけなさい。
English: Now, then, witness. Continue with your testimony.

Japanese: たしか、事故は深夜 起こったんでしたっけ‥‥？
English: The accident happened at night, correct...?

Japanese: そうですねえ。 夜中の１時ぐらいですう。
English: Yeah, like, it was at, like, 1 AM or so, I think.

Japanese: 私、論文のしめきりが 近かったから‥‥
English: I had, like, a paper due, like, really soon...

Japanese: しばらく、寝ないで がんばってたんですう。
English: so, like, I hadn't slept and was working, like, real hard.

Japanese: ２人は眠りこんでしまい‥‥ そして事故が起こった。
English: So it was because the two of you were so tired that this accident happened.

Japanese: そのとき、お姉さんは‥‥？
English: And your sister? What happened to her?

Japanese: ‥‥見えなかったんですぅ。
English: I... I didn't see her...

Japanese: 車の中はもう、まっ黒な ケムリでいっぱいで‥‥
English: The car was, like, totally black, and there was, like, a ton of smoke...

Japanese: もう私、何がなんだか わからなくなっちゃってぇ。
English: I, like, totally had, like, no clue what was going on.

Japanese: そのときのこと、 くわしく話してください。
English: Please tell us about your escape in more detail.

Japanese: 私‥‥ずっと忘れようと してましたからあ、 I...
English: I, like, kept wanting to forget that time,

Japanese: もう、そのときのことは ハッキリしないんですう。
English: so, like, I don't remember things about the accident in, like, detail anymore...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ ムリもありませんねえ。
English: Hmm, well, it's alright. No need to push yourself.

Japanese: たしか、事故のあと インタビューされたんですう。
English: I think I, like, gave an interview after the accident.

Japanese: それが一番、たしかだと 思うんですけどお。
English: Like, that would probably be the most accurate, you know?

Japanese: （‥‥その記事なら ぼくも持っていたな‥‥）
English: (Hmm, if it's that article, then I've already got it...)

Japanese: それで、あなたは助手席に‥‥？
English: And that's why you were in the passenger's seat?

Japanese: ‥‥はあい。
English: Like, yeah.

Japanese: 私も大学の研究室に おそくまでいたからぁ‥‥、
English: I was, like, at my college's research lab 'till, like, really late...

Japanese: 送ってもらったんですう。
English: So my sis was, like, giving me a ride home.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどね。
English: I see...

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: は、はい？
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: ‥‥ダメよ。 すんなりナットクしちゃ‥‥
English: You can't do this. You're being too easy on her.

Japanese: ‥‥のどかさん。 今のウソは、おそまつでしたね。
English: Ms. Miney. That was a lame lie just now.

Japanese: どういうこと‥‥ですかあ？
English: Like, what do you mean?

Japanese: あなたは免許を 持っていたはずです！
English: I know you had a license back then!

Japanese: これは、その免許証のために 撮った写真ですね！
English: This is a photo you took for the express purpose of getting a driver's license!

Japanese: あッ‥‥
English: Umm...

Japanese: ‥‥証人！ どういうことですか！
English: What is the meaning of this, Ms. Miney!?

Japanese: ‥‥あ、あの‥‥ そ、そうですぅ。
English: Ah, um... Uh... That's... That's right!

Japanese: た、たしかに私、 免許を持ってますけどお‥‥
English: Y-Yeah, I had, like, a license... But...

Japanese: でも、交付されたのは あの事故のあとなんですよお。
English: But I didn't get it until after the accident!

Japanese: そんなはずはありません！
English: No, you had it.

Japanese: あなたは、事故があったとき、 すでに免許を持っていた！
English: You had it at the time of the accident!

Japanese: 堀田院長‥‥らしきヒトが そう言ってました！
English: Director Hotti... or the guy pretending to be him anyway, said so!

Japanese: ‥‥あの、すけべな ニセ院長のこと？
English: What, are you talking about that perverted fake clinic director?

Japanese: そのすけべなニセ院長です。 しかし、すけべだからといって‥‥
English: Yes, that perverted fake clinic director. And how did you know he was perverted...

Japanese: あうっ！
English: Eowch!

Japanese: ‥‥そんなコトは どうでもよろしい。
English: No one cares.

Japanese: 証人。あなたの免許証が 発行されたのは、いつなの？
English: Witness. When did you receive your driver's license?

Japanese: 去年の１１月ですぅ。
English: Like, last November.
What? N-N-November...?

Japanese: 交通事故があったのは５月。 ‥‥その半年後、ということね。
English: The accident happened last May. That's a half year lapse in time, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: どどどどど、どういうことだ！
English: Wh-Wh-Wh-Wh-What in the!?

Japanese: そそそそそ、そういうことです 成歩堂くん！
English: Th-Th-Th-Th-That's what happened, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 事故があったとき、証人はまだ 免許を持っていなかった！
English: At the time of the accident, Ms. Miney did not have a driver's license.

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Uuuuurk...

Japanese: いてェ！
English: OW!

Japanese: ‥‥泣きっツラにムチ。
English: Cry, and my whip will accommodate.

Japanese: とにかく、免許がないのでは、 運転をかわれるワケがありません。
English: Anyway, without a license, the witness and her sister could not have switched drivers.

Japanese: わかってもらえれば いいんですけどお。
English: I'm, like, glad you get it, but...

Japanese: でも、たぶん私が免許を持って いたとしても、
English: like, even if I had my license,

Japanese: 運転させてもらえなかった と思いますけどねえ‥‥
English: I, like, don't think my sis woulda, like, let me drive.

Japanese: わちゃッ！
English: Yeow!

Japanese: “ふうん”じゃないでしょ！ なるほどくん！
English: Don't just stand there "hmm"-ing to yourself!

Japanese: ちち、千尋さんまで ムチを‥‥ッ！
English: Not you too, Mia! With the whip... And the pain... And the oww...

Japanese: 証人！　なぜ、運転させて もらえなかったと思うのですか？
English: Ms. Miney! Why do you think you would not have been allowed to drive anyway?

Japanese: え？　ええと‥‥それはぁ、
English: Eh? Um... That's because...

Japanese: ついでですから、証人。 そのことを証言してください。
English: I think this situation calls for a more detailed testimony. Ms. Miney, if you please.

Japanese: えー。めんどくさぁい。
English: Eh? Like, how annoying.

Japanese: よけいなコト、 言っちゃいましたねぇ‥‥。
English: Oops, like, sorry. Didn't mean to be mean...

Japanese: ～運転をかわらなかった理由～
English: -- I Wouldn't Be Allowed --

Japanese: あのころ、私、もうちょっとで 免許をもらえるところでしたぁ。
English: Like, around that time, I was, like, really close to getting my license.

Japanese: お姉さん、クルマをものすごく ダイジにしてたんですう。
English: My sis was, like, this totally big fan of cars and, like, really valued them.

Japanese: とどいたばかりの新車でしたぁ。 まっ赤なスポーツカー。
English: She, like, had just gotten this really shiny, bright red sports car.

Japanese: 「新人ドライバーには運転させて あげないよ」‥‥って。
English: She, like, would say things like, "No way am I letting a newb drive my car!"

Japanese: だから、あの夜も、私、 助手席に乗ってましたぁ。
English: So, like, that's why I ended up in the passenger's seat that night too.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥なるほど。 まっかなすぽーつかー、ですか。
English: Hmm... I see... A bright red car for sports...

Japanese: ‥‥では、 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Now then, the defense may question the witness.

Japanese: （ううん‥‥ クルマはニガテなんだよな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I'm not terribly knowledgeable about cars, but...)

Japanese: ～運転をかわらなかった理由～
English: -- I Wouldn't Be Allowed --

Japanese: あのころ、私、もうちょっとで 免許をもらえるところでしたぁ。
English: Like, around that time, I was, like, really close to getting my license.

Japanese: お姉さん、クルマをものすごく ダイジにしてたんですう。
English: My sis was, like, this totally big fan of cars and, like, really valued them.

Japanese: 「新人ドライバーには運転させて あげないよ」‥‥って。
English: She, like, would say things like, "No way am I letting a newb drive my car!"

Japanese: だから、あの夜も、私、 助手席に乗ってましたぁ。
English: So, like, that's why I ended up in the passenger's seat that night too.

Japanese: ‥‥ここが最大のポイントね。
English: There is something very big here.

Japanese: こういう自然な証言は、 くずすのがむずかしいわ。
English: It's going to be hard to break apart such a natural-sounding testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥じっくり攻めましょう。 かならず、どこかに穴がある。
English: But we have to attack it. And by doing so, a flaw will show itself. It has to.

Japanese: そ、そうでしょうか。
English: D-Do you really think so?

Japanese: 信じなさい。そこに道があるわ。
English: Believe. There is a path, I know it.

Japanese: （うう‥‥千尋さんが 神になっていく‥‥）
English: (Hmm... Mia almost sounded like a prophet for a second there...)

Japanese: お姉さんの車、アメリカ製で 特別仕様の新車だったんですぅ。
English: My sis' new car was, like, a totally special model from England.

Japanese: とどいたばかりの新車でしたぁ。 まっ赤なスポーツカー。
English: She, like, had just gotten this really shiny, bright red sports car.

Japanese: もうちょっと‥‥というのは？
English: So what do you mean by, "really close"?

Japanese: あの、“仮免”ていうんですかぁ？ アレをもらえるはずだったんです。
English: Like, I had one of those "permit" things. I think that's what they're called.

Japanese: はあ。かりめん、ですか。
English: Um, "permit"?

Japanese: うわ。弁護士のくせに 仮免も知らないんですかぁ？
English: Wow, the big name lawyer boy doesn't even know what a driver's permit is!?

Japanese: べ、弁護士に仮免は ありませんから。
English: Well, this lawyer never had a permit.

Japanese: うわっ！
English: Wah!

Japanese: ‥‥無知にはムチを。
English: Ignorance is to be whipped.

Japanese: （なんか、さっきから ビミョーなコト言うよな‥‥）
English: (What is with her...? All she's said in the last few minutes is utter nonsense...)

Japanese: いいですかぁ？　つづけて。
English: So, like, may I continue?

Japanese: “ダイジに”って‥‥ どれぐらいですか？
English: She "valued" cars? How much would you say she valued her car?

Japanese: それはもお。 雨のしずくはモチロンのこと、
English: Well, like, she'd flip out at, like, a drop of rain...

Japanese: きたない手でドアを さわるのも気にしてましたぁ。
English: and, like, she would notice if someone, like, touched the car door with dirty hands.

Japanese: （それなら、そもそも 車庫から出さなけりゃいいのに）
English: (In that case, why bother taking the car out of the garage, ever!?)

Japanese: ‥‥どうして、そんなに ダイジにしてたんですか？
English: Why did she take such special care of the car?

Japanese: ナニ言ってるんですかぁ、 新車だったんですよぉ。
English: How can you say that!? It was a brand-spanking new car!

Japanese: ‥‥新車？
English: ...New car?

Japanese: 弁護士さんだって、 よごしたくないでしょお？
English: Like, you wouldn't want to, like, get it dirty, would you, Mr. Lawyer?

Japanese: （クルマなんて、よごれて トーゼンだと思うけど‥‥）
English: (I would think that cars getting dirty is just another fact of life, but...)

Japanese: （きっと、クルマ好きは そうは思わないんだろうな）
English: (I guess people who really love cars think otherwise...)

Japanese: なるほどくん。‥‥もっと イミのあることを聞きなさい。
English: Phoenix, try asking something of more significance, alright?

Japanese: それで？　証人。 どんなクルマだったんですか？
English: Well, Ms. Miney? What kind of car was it?

Japanese: （クルマのことは よくわからないからな‥‥）
English: (I guess I just don't get what's so special about cars...)

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。その 車について証言してください。
English: Alright, then, please testify about your sister's car.

Japanese: 新車‥‥ですか。
English: It was a new car...?

Japanese: ええ。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: とどいたばっかりだったんですよ。 ‥‥アメリカから。
English: She had, like, just gotten it. It was, like, from the U.K.

Japanese: は？　‥‥あめりか‥‥
English: Hm? The "U.K."? Was that her boyfriend's initials?

Japanese: しかも、特別限定モデルだった んですよお。
English: And it was a really special model.

Japanese: とどくまで、１年ぐらい 待ってたんですからねえ。
English: She had waited for a whole year for it to arrive.

Japanese: （クルマ好きには たまらない話題なんだろうな）
English: (I guess her love of cars would be the obvious next topic...)

Japanese: （クルマのことは よくわからないけど‥‥）
English: (But I really don't know anything about cars...)

Japanese: （どうもこれは、ムネに キュンと来るモノがある！）
English: (But I had a feeling this was bound to come up!)

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 では、つづきを‥‥
English: I see... Please continue...

Japanese: 待って！　なるほどくん。 この証言は聞き流してはダメよ！
English: Hold on! Phoenix! You can't just ignore this!

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥そんなに クルマ好きでしたっけ？
English: Mia... I had no idea you liked cars so much too...

Japanese: そういう問題じゃないわ。
English: That's not my point!

Japanese: わ、わかりました！
English: O-Oh, yeah!

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: なんですか？
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: 今の証人の発言を、 証言に加えてください！
English: Please append what the witness has just said to her testimony!

Japanese: 特別仕様の外車のくだりですか？
English: You mean the part about it being a special car from overseas?

Japanese: はい！
English: Yes!

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。クルマ好きは これだから‥‥
English: *sigh* Well, if you like cars that much, then...

Japanese: そういう問題じゃありません！
English: That's not my point!

Japanese: （‥‥と言っても、どういう問題か ぼくもよくわからないけど‥‥）
English: (Though to be honest, I don't know what the point is either...)

Japanese: ‥‥証人。証言を 変えていただけますか？
English: Ms. Miney, would you please fix your testimony?

Japanese: いいですけどぉ‥‥。
English: Sure, whatever.

Japanese: お姉さん、運転は うまかったんですか？
English: Was your sister good at driving?

Japanese: それはもう、スゴかった ですよぉ。
English: She was, like, totally awesome!

Japanese: “看護師界のレースクイーンを めざす”って言ってましたぁ。
English: She, like, said she was going to be the, like, "Racing Queen of the Nursing World".

Japanese: （‥‥それ、なんかイミが ちがうような気がするぞ‥‥）
English: (...Somehow, I think she bumped my question off-track again...)

Japanese: そこで、事故にあった わけですね‥‥？
English: And that was when the accident occurred, correct?

Japanese: そうですぅ。とっさに 助手席のドアを開けて‥‥
English: Like, yeah... I, like, immediately reached for the passenger side door,

Japanese: ムチュウで飛びだし ましたあ。
English: and fled, like, in a daze...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ タイヘンでしたねえ。
English: Hmm... Sounds like you had a terrifying time...

Japanese: （イセイよく“待った！”と 言ってはみたけど‥‥）
English: (I yelled, "Hold it" pretty forcefully, but...)

Japanese: （クルマのことなんて、 聞くことがないぞ‥‥）
English: (I don't actually know what to ask about cars...)

Japanese: ええと‥‥そうですね。 （何か話題、ワダイ‥‥と）
English: Um... So, yes... (What am I supposed to pick for a topic...?)

Japanese: そもそも、どうして アメリカのクルマがいいんですか？
English: Let's start with this. Why are British cars good?

Japanese: はあ？
English: Like, huh?

Japanese: お金はかかりそうだし、 運転席は日本車と逆だし‥‥
English: They're expensive, and their driver's seat is opposite to our cars here in the States.

Japanese: いいところ、まるで なさそうですけどねえ。
English: It would seem there is no benefit to owning one.

Japanese: な、なんですって‥‥？
English: Like... Huh?

Japanese: 今どき“左ハンドル”でジマン、 ってのも、どうなんでしょう。
English: This whole steering wheel on the right side thing; it's kind of goofy, isn't it?

Japanese: あ‥‥アンタなんかには わかんないのよッ！
English: Y-You! What do you know!?

Japanese: あのセンサイなフォルム！ かれたエンジン音！
English: That sleek shape! The purr of the engine!

Japanese: ワイルドなスタート！ それでいてスムーズな加速！
English: The wild way it starts! And its smooth handling!

Japanese: ギアシフトのなめらかさ！ すずしいクーラーの風！
English: The raw feeling of manual transmission! The cool breeze of the AC!

Japanese: うう‥‥ごめんなさい‥‥
English: Uuugh... Forgive me...

Japanese: （この証人、ホント、いきなり 性格が変わるよなあ‥‥）
English: (Her personality really changes at the drop of a hat...)

Japanese: おもしろいわね、なるほどくん。
English: It's interesting, isn't it, Phoenix?

Japanese: ‥‥だんだん見えてきたわ。 この事件の、本当のムジュンが。
English: Bit by bit, we're beginning to see the real "contradiction" behind this case.

Japanese: （どうも、クルマの話題は ニガテだ‥‥）
English: (I really stink at talking about cars...)

Japanese: （ヘタに質問して 怒らせるのもなんだし‥‥）
English: (If I ask the wrong question, I might make someone mad...)

Japanese: では、証人。 ‥‥証言をつづけてください。
English: Ms. Miney, I believe you may continue with your testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥のどかさん。
English: Ms. Miney.

Japanese: この記事、おぼえていますか？
English: Do you remember this article?

Japanese: 交通事故の記事。 あなたの証言がのっています。
English: It's an article about the accident. You had said this in it:

Japanese: “左のドアを開けて、クルマから 転がり出た”‥‥
English: "But I opened the right door, and, like, got out"...

Japanese: まちがいありませんね？
English: That's correct, isn't it?

Japanese: な‥‥なんで急に そんなものを‥‥
English: Like, why are you suddenly asking me about that...?

Japanese: 裁判長。‥‥クルマの助手席は、 左右どちら側にありますか？
English: Your Honor. Which side is the passenger's seat: the left or the right?

Japanese: それはもちろん‥‥左側でしょう。 運転席が右側ですからね。
English: Well, it's on the right side, of course, because the driver's on the left side.

Japanese: それは、日本車の場合です。 アメリカのクルマは‥‥逆です。
English: That would be if it were an American car. But it would be the opposite in a British car.

Japanese: ぎゃく‥‥
English: Opposite...?

Japanese: ２人の乗っていたクルマは アメリカ製だった！
English: The two of them were riding in a British car!

Japanese: この場合、助手席は 右側になります！
English: In that case, the passenger side would be the left side!

Japanese: ‥‥ああああっ！
English: Aaaaah!

Japanese: ど、どういうことですかッ！
English: Wh-What do you mean by this!?

Japanese: のどかさん！　あなたは 左のドアから逃げたと言う！
English: Ms. Miney! You said that you had escaped using the right side door of the car!

Japanese: それならば！ あなたが座っていたのは‥‥
English: But if you were sitting there,

Japanese: 運転席だったことになる！
English: then that means you were in the driver's seat!

Japanese: きゃああアアッ！
English: Aaaaaaaah!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！ 静粛に！　静粛にィィッ！
English: O-Order! Order in the court! Order! Ooooooooorder!!

Japanese: 静粛‥‥
English: Order...

Japanese: かはッ！
English: Ack!

Japanese: ナニを言いだすの！
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright is blabbering nonsense again!

Japanese: 運転していたのは、姉のほう ‥‥葉中　未実なのよ！
English: Ini said the person who was driving was her older sister, Mimi Miney!

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。 話にスジが通らない。
English: That is what Ini said, yes But that is where her story falls apart.

Japanese: つまり、ぼくたちはどこかで カンちがいをしてるんですよ！
English: Somewhere, all of us made a big error in our assumptions!

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: やっと‥‥見えたわね。 この事件の真相が！
English: It looks like you've finally found the real root of this murder!

Japanese: すべての要素をつなげて考えれば、 答えは１つしかない！
English: If we connect all the dots, there can only be one answer!

Japanese: 問題は！　姉と妹‥‥ どちらが運転席にいたのか？
English: I'd like to ask the court a question. Ini or Mimi -- who was really driving that night?

Japanese: もちろん、葉中　のどかが 運転していたのです！
English: And the answer is Ini Miney, of course!

Japanese: どど、どうして そうなるのですか！
English: Wh-Why would you say that?

Japanese: たった今、証言されたばかりです！
English: Ms. Ini Miney just said so herself in her testimony!

Japanese: 証人は、左のドアから 逃げだした！
English: She said that she escaped the car from the right side door!

Japanese: つまり、運転席です！
English: Which would have been where the driver's seat was!

Japanese: いてェェェッ！
English: Owwwww!

Japanese: バカのバカが度をすぎると バカではすまなくなる。
English: I'm inclined to feel sorry for a foolish fool who foolishly spends his time foolishly.

Japanese: 当時、葉中　のどかはまだ 免許を持っていなかったのよ！
English: At that time, Ms. Ini Miney had not yet received her driver's license!

Japanese: だからこそ、交通事故が 起こったんですよ！
English: And that is why the accident happened!

Japanese: 待って！　なるほどくん。
English: Hold it, Phoenix!

Japanese: どうやらあなたには、 まだ真相が見えていないようね。
English: It looks like you still don't see the real truth behind the accident.
Th-The "real truth"...?

Japanese: 発想を逆転させるの。 ‥‥私たちの合いコトバでしょう！
English: Think outside the box! Isn't that what we always say?

Japanese: （も、もう一度 考えてみるか‥‥！）
English: (A-Alright, one more time...!)

Japanese: 運転免許を持っていたのは、 姉の葉中　未実です。
English: The answer is the one person who had her driver's license, Mimi Miney.

Japanese: ならば当然、運転していたのは 彼女でなければならない！
English: After all, that makes her the only person who could legally drive!

Japanese: し‥‥しかし、 あなたは証明したばかりです！
English: B-But, you just said...

Japanese: この証人が‥‥のどかさんが、 運転席にいたことを！
English: The witness, Ms. Ini Miney, was the one in the driver's seat!

Japanese: ‥‥それが、次の問題です。
English: Which leads us to the next question...

Japanese: ですって！
English: 次の‥‥<von Karma> 問題‥‥<Judge> Th-The<von Karma> next<Judge> question!?

Japanese: その証人台に立っている人物は いったい、だれなのか‥‥？
English: Who is the person standing on the witness stand right now?
Uuughn!

Japanese: な‥‥ナニを言いだすの！ 成歩堂　龍一‥‥
English: What sort of idiotic ruse is this, Mr. Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: こ、この証人は‥‥ この証人の名前は‥‥
English: Th-This witness' name... This witness' name is...

Japanese: ぼくが教えてあげましょう。
English: I'll tell you what her name really is.

Japanese: ‥‥この証人の名前は！
English: This witness' real name is...!

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥ちなみに、 あなたの名前はなんですか？
English: Would the defense please inform the court of its birth name?

Japanese: え。‥‥な、成歩堂　龍一 ですけど‥‥
English: Umm... Phoenix Wright... Your Honor.

Japanese: ふう。ちょっと安心しました。
English: Ah, what a relief. I thought you'd forgotten your own name... Again.

Japanese: 私も、ちょっと安心しました。
English: I was beginning to think he forgot too.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら ハズしたみたいですね。
English: Wait a second... I screwed up didn't I?

Japanese: 大きく、ね。
English: Big time.

Japanese: ‥‥いい？　今が大詰めなの。 気を抜いたら、すべてがパアよ。
English: Listen. This is the grand finale. Don't hold back! Let it all out on this one!

Japanese: もも、もう一度 やらせてください！
English: A-Alright, I'll give it another shot!

Japanese: ‥‥葉中　未実。 それがあなたの、本当の名前です。
English: Mimi Miney. That is your real name.

Japanese: バ、バカ言わないで！
English: Of all the foolish dribble!

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ この写真はいったいなんなの！
English: Th-Then, how do you explain her appearance!?

Japanese: この証人は、入院したとき、 カオに大ヤケドをしていました。
English: When she was admitted into the hospital, this witness was suffering severe facial burns.

Japanese: そのため、復元の整形手術を 受けています！
English: So for the purpose of facial reconstruction, they used a picture.

Japanese: せ‥‥整形手術‥‥！
English: Facial reconstruction surgery!?

Japanese: そのとき、彼女は この写真をわたしたんです。
English: This is the picture she gave to her doctors at the time.

Japanese: そう！　妹の写真をね！
English: That's right! It's a picture of her younger sister!

Japanese: ‥‥きゃあああっ！
English: Nooooo!

Japanese: し‥‥しかし！　葉中　未実は 死んだはず‥‥交通事故で！
English: B-But... Mimi Miney... She died... In the car accident!

Japanese: みんなそう思っていました。 しかし‥‥そうではなかった。
English: That's what everyone thought. However, that was not the case.

Japanese: じゃ、‥‥じゃあ、 事故現場で見つかった死体は‥‥
English: Th-Then, the body they found at the crash site...

Japanese: 葉中　のどかさんだったのです。 ‥‥ホンモノのね。
English: That was the body of the real Ini Miney.

Japanese: いかがですか！ ‥‥葉中　未実さん！
English: Isn't that right... Ms. Mimi Miney!?

Japanese: （１年前の交通事故‥‥）
English: (That car accident one year ago...)

Japanese: （死んだのは、葉中　のどかの ほうだった）
English: (The one who died that night was Ini Miney.)

Japanese: （姉の未実は、妹のカオに 整形して‥‥生まれ変わった）
English: (Her sister, Mimi, then stole her face... And was reborn as Ini.)

Japanese: （そして、“葉中　未実”の存在を ‥‥自ら消そうとしたんだ！）
English: (With this, she effectively erased "Mimi Miney" from existence!)

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥これで、なぜ霧崎先生が殺害 されたか、おわかりでしょう。
English: I'm sure you can now see why Mimi had to kill Dr. Grey.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Wh-What do you mean?

Japanese: はわっ！
English: Aaah!

Japanese: どういうことッ！ 成歩堂　龍一！
English: What is the meaning of this nonsense!?

Japanese: 霧崎医師は、死者を 呼び出そうとしていました。
English: Dr. Grey wanted to call back the spirit of a dead person.

Japanese: 事故で死んだ看護師 ‥‥葉中　未実の霊を。
English: Specifically, the spirit of his nurse that died in the crash, Mimi Miney.

Japanese: しかし、そんなこと できるわけがない！
English: However, that would not have been possible!

Japanese: なぜなら、“葉中　未実”は 生きているからです！
English: Because "Mimi Miney" was still, in fact, alive!

Japanese: ‥‥霊媒をされては、 それがバレてしまいます。
English: And that fact would have been discovered had the channeling been conducted.

Japanese: この証人は、それだけは 防がなければならなかった！
English: So this witness had to stop that from happening...

Japanese: ‥‥なんとしても！
English: ...at all costs!

Japanese: だ‥‥だから‥‥
English: And... And...

Japanese: だから、殺害したと言うの？ ‥‥霧崎　哲郎を‥‥
English: And that's why she had to kill Dr. Turner Grey? Is that what you're saying!?

Japanese: ‥‥そうです。
English: Yes.

Japanese: 霊媒が始まる前に、ね。
English: Before the channeling,

Japanese: 霧崎先生は不幸にも、霊媒の相談を この証人に持ちかけてしまった。
English: Dr. Grey had the misfortune of consulting this witness about communicating with the dead.

Japanese: その瞬間‥‥ 霧崎先生の運命は決まったのです！
English: And in that moment, his fate was sealed!

Japanese: いかがですか？ のどか‥‥イヤ、かるま‥‥
English: Well, Ms. Ini...? I mean, Ms. von Karma...

Japanese: はひッ！
English: Eek!

Japanese: ‥‥なぜそこで 私の名前が出てくるのっ！
English: Why did you say MY name, just now!?

Japanese: やられた‥‥わね。
English: Looks like I've been unmasked.
W-Witness...?

Japanese: そうよ。あたしの名前は ‥‥葉中　未実。
English: That's right, I admit it. My real name is Mimi Miney.

Japanese: ‥‥あのヤブ医者、ケッキョク 最後までコケにしてくれたわ。
English: That quack doctor... He got what he deserved in the end.

Japanese: もうちょっとで‥‥ 未実を捨てることができたのに。
English: And I was so close to finally ditching "Mimi" too.

Japanese: 本当に‥‥、 もうちょっとだった‥‥
English: So close...

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥なぜです！
English: But... But why!?

Japanese: どうしてそこまでして、 葉中　未実を‥‥
English: Why would you go so far to throw away your self...?

Japanese: 妹さんになりすますなんて！
English: To become your own sister!?

Japanese: （ぼくには、なんとなく わかるような気がする‥‥）
English: (I think I understand why...)

Japanese: （葉中　未実が自分を 消そうとした理由は‥‥）
English: (Mimi Miney wanted her self to disappear because of this...)

Japanese: ‥‥やめておきなさい。
English: Just stop.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ハジをかくだけよ。 ‥‥その証拠を提出しても。
English: I feel embarrassed for you for even thinking of showing that piece of evidence.

Japanese: ‥‥彼女は、 すべてを認めたわ。
English: That woman... She had lost everything.

Japanese: それでヨシとしておく しかないようね、なるほどくん。
English: So this was the only way to make things right, Phoenix.

Japanese: （ちがったみたいだな‥‥ この証拠‥‥）
English: (Looks like this wasn't the right piece of evidence after all...)

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、ぼくはこの事件を 理解できなかったみたいだ‥‥）
English: (I guess I don't fully understand this case yet...)

Japanese: ‥‥そ、それは‥‥
English: Th-That's...

Japanese: 医療ミス‥‥ッ！
English: That's about the malpractice incident!

Japanese: 霧崎先生は正しかったんですよ。
English: Dr. Grey was right.

Japanese: あのミスは、ある看護師によって 引き起こされました。
English: The mistake was caused by the nurse.

Japanese: ‥‥葉中　未実という名の 看護師によって。
English: A nurse by the name of Mimi Miney.

Japanese: そう。今、証言台に立っている 人物です。
English: That's right, the person standing before us today.

Japanese: ‥‥そのミスから、 たった数週間後‥‥
English: And a few weeks after this mishap,

Japanese: 葉中　未実は、今度は交通事故を 起こしてしまった。
English: Mimi Miney had a car accident,

Japanese: そして‥‥妹が死にました。
English: in which she lost her younger sister.

Japanese: さ‥‥サイアクのタイミング‥‥
English: Th-The timing couldn't have been worse...

Japanese: 事故とはいえ、１４人の患者と 妹の生命をうばってしまった。
English: Fourteen patients dying in that incident, and her own sister's life, extinguished.

Japanese: ‥‥背負って生きていくには、 重すぎたのです。
English: It was all too much to bear.

Japanese: ‥‥逃れる方法があったわ。 たった、１つだけ‥‥。
English: ...I found a way for myself to escape it all... The only way...

Japanese: 事故は、すべてを焼きつくした。 妹の亡きがらも、自らのカオも。
English: She lost everything in the flames of that accident, her sister...

Japanese: ‥‥それこそが、 最後のチャンスだったのです。
English: ...and even her own face. This was her last chance...

Japanese: 過去を捨てて、“妹”として 新しい人生を始めるチャンス‥‥
English: Her chance to throw away her past and start a new life as her "sister"...

Japanese: ‥‥想像を絶する事件ですな‥‥
English: ...Unbelievable... A plan beyond my wildest imagination...

Japanese: アイツが‥‥
English: That jerk...

Japanese: 霊媒なんてくだらないコト、 思いつきさえしなければ‥‥
English: If only he hadn't thought of that channeling mumbo-jumbo...

Japanese: くだらないコト‥‥？
English: ...Mumbo-jumbo?

Japanese: あたしが“のどか”になって、 一番イヤだったコト。
English: Becoming Ini has been the most horrible experience in the world.

Japanese: 霊媒‥‥オカルト‥‥ そんなのあたし、大ッキライ。
English: Spirit channeling, the occult... I hate it! I hate it all!

Japanese: ‥‥いくつかのナゾは、 残されたままです。
English: ...I believe there are still a few unsolved riddles here...

Japanese: なぜ、こんなフクザツな 殺人計画が実行されたのか？
English: Why did you set a murderous revenge plot into motion?

Japanese: 綾里　キミ子は、なぜ 葉中　未実に協力したのか‥‥？
English: And why did Ms. Morgan Fey agree to help you with the plan...?

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。１つだけ、 ハッキリしたことがあります。
English: Regardless, I believe one thing has been made crystal clear.

Japanese: 被告人・綾里　真宵の無実‥‥
English: The innocence of the defendant, Maya Fey...

Japanese: こ‥‥こんな‥‥
English: Th-This...

Japanese: こんなバカなッ！
English: This is preposterous!

Japanese: 私は‥‥私は カンペキだったはず！
English: I... I'm perfect!

Japanese: この私が‥‥狩魔　冥が‥‥
English: Me... Franziska von Karma...

Japanese: ‥‥今夜のニュースが楽しみ だね、狩魔検事。
English: I'm going to enjoy the news tonight, Ms. von Karma. How about you?

Japanese: 全世界に流れるんだろ？ ‥‥キミの敗北するすがたが。
English: It's going to be broadcast all over the world, right? Your defeat, that is...

Japanese: あちっ！
English: Yeow!

Japanese: ひひいッ！
English: Ohhh!

Japanese: あぎゃッ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: いてててていてていててててていて ててていててていててていてていて
English: Owowowowwwowoowowowoww owwwowwowwwooowowwooow owowoowooowowowoowooww

Japanese: そして、トドメよッ！
English: And one more for good measure!

Japanese: なな、なるほどくん！ しっかりして！　なるほどくん！
English: Ph-Phoenix! Hang in there, Phoenix!! PHOENIX!!!

Japanese: こんな裁判、認められないわ！
English: This court is a fraud! A sham!

Japanese: ‥‥それでは。
English: ...Now then,

Japanese: 弁護人が目をさますまで、 もう少しかかりそうなので‥‥
English: it looks like it will be some time before Mr. Wright regains consciousness...

Japanese: 先に、綾里　真宵に対する 判決を言いわたします。
English: so I will go ahead and pronounce the verdict.

Japanese: ‥‥では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!

Japanese: 同日　午後　３時１３分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: June 22, 3:13 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: ‥‥おめでとう、真宵。
English: Congratulations, Maya.

Japanese: おっ！　お姉ちゃん！
English: S-Sis!

Japanese: ‥‥ひさしぶりね、真宵。 元気だった？
English: It's good to see you, Maya... How are you doing?

Japanese: お姉ちゃん！　お姉ちゃあん‥‥
English: Sis! Sis...

Japanese: あたし‥‥あたし、 だれも殺してないんだよね？
English: I-I didn't kill anyone, did I?

Japanese: そうよ。あなたは、 夢を見ていただけ。
English: No, you didn't. It was all just a dream...

Japanese: 悪い夢を、ね‥‥
English: A really bad dream.

Japanese: ‥‥あのね、お姉ちゃん。
English: You know, sis...

Japanese: 夢の中でね。 なつかしいニオイがしたんだよ。
English: In my dream, I smelled a really familiar scent.

Japanese: なつかしい‥‥？
English: A familiar scent...?

Japanese: あたし、衣装ばこの中に 入れられてたんでしょ？
English: I was inside that clothing box, right?

Japanese: あの衣装ばこ‥‥
English: That box...

Japanese: お姉ちゃんの、むかしの 装束がしまってあったんだよ。
English: That was the box you used to store your clothes in a long time ago...

Japanese: ‥‥そうだったの‥‥
English: That's right!

Japanese: ‥‥１つ、わからないことが あるんですよ。
English: Hmm... There is still one thing I don't quite get.

Japanese: なあに？
English: What's that?

Japanese: 霧崎先生が撃たなかったら‥‥ どうなっていたんでしょう？
English: What would've happened if Mimi hadn't shot Dr. Grey...?

Japanese: 銃声を聞いたから、ぼくたちは 現場に踏みこんだんですから。
English: I mean, we broke in because we heard a gunshot.

Japanese: たぶん計画では、葉中　未実自身が カギを開けるはずだったのよ。
English: I think Mimi Miney had planned to open the door to the Chamber herself.

Japanese: そして、あなたとナツミさんに 目撃させたはずよ。
English: And then, you and Lotta would have witnessed quite a scene;

Japanese: “霊の乗りうつった真宵が 殺人を犯したシーン”をね。
English: a "possessed" Maya Fey, who had just committed a murder.

Japanese: ‥‥細かいところまで、 計算されていたんですね‥‥
English: She had it planned down to the smallest detail, huh...?

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: おめでとう。真宵ちゃん。
English: Congrats, Maya.

Japanese: ありがとう！
English: Thanks!

Japanese: ‥‥また、 助けてもらっちゃったね。
English: Looks like you bailed me out of another jam.

Japanese: まあ、ね。
English: Well... you know...

Japanese: ‥‥でも。
English: But...

Japanese: ホントにもう、こんなの、 二度とヤだな。
English: I really don't want something like this to happen ever again.

Japanese: あたしはいいよ。なるほどくんが 助けてくれるから。
English: I'm fine because I have you to help me out, Nick...

Japanese: ‥‥でも、事件が起こると、 大切な人がいなくなるの。
English: But every time something happens, I lose someone special to me.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんも‥‥そして、 おばさまも。
English: First my sister... And now my aunt...

Japanese: ねえ、なるほどくん。教えて！
English: Hey, Nick... Tell me...

Japanese: どうしておばさま、 こんなヒドいことをしたの？
English: Tell me why my aunt went and did something so horrible?

Japanese: あんな計画に‥‥、 未実さんに手を貸すなんて‥‥！
English: Why would she help Ms. Mimi with a plan like that...!?

Japanese: あたし、ホントに ‥‥こわかったんだよ？
English: I just... I just don't understand it, Nick...

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥。
English: Maya, it's over. Why don't we just let it be.

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥ 教えてくれるよね？
English: Nick, please, I need an answer...

Japanese: （キミ子さんが、葉中　未実に 手を貸した理由‥‥それは‥‥）
English: (The reason Morgan helped Mimi with her plan was...)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥？ どういうコト？
English: I don't get it.

Japanese: キミ子さんがどうして、 葉中　未実に協力したか？
English: Yeah, me neither.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなこと、 ぼくにはわからないよ。
English: Even I don't really know why Morgan cooperated with Mimi Miney...

Japanese: 事件は終わったんだ。 ‥‥もう、考えないで。
English: Look, this whole thing is over now, so let's not dwell on the past, OK?

Japanese: ‥‥うん。わかった。 ありがとう、なるほどくん。
English: Yeah... Thanks, Nick.

Japanese: （‥‥証拠品、どうやら ちがったみたいだけど‥‥）
English: (I just don't know the answer to this one...)

Japanese: （なんだ、 “ありがとう”って‥‥？）
English: (I'm just as confused about the situation as she is... So why the "thanks"?)

Japanese: ‥‥はみちゃん？
English: Pearly?

Japanese: 今から４年後‥‥倉院の里には、 新しい家元が生まれる。
English: Four years from now, a new Master will be "born" into Kurain Village.

Japanese: ‥‥きみだよ、真宵ちゃん。
English: And that person will be you, Maya.

Japanese: そ、それが‥‥？
English: A-And...?

Japanese: でも、もしきみが いなくなったら‥‥
English: But, if you weren't there...

Japanese: 本家の血は絶えてしまう。 ‥‥そのときは？
English: then the main family's blood- line would disappear... And what would happen then...?

Japanese: 分家の‥‥おばさまが‥‥
English: ...The branch family... My aunt...

Japanese: ‥‥ちがう。キミ子さんの霊力は そんなに強くない。
English: ...No, Morgan's spiritual power is too weak.

Japanese: つぐのは‥‥春美ちゃんだ。
English: The next person would be... Pearls.

Japanese: ‥‥あああああっ‥‥！
English: ...Aaaah!

Japanese: すべては、そのために。
English: Everything was done for her sake.

Japanese: 春美ちゃんを家元に するためだったんだよ。
English: It was all so that Pearls would become the next Master.

Japanese: そ、そうだったんだね‥‥。
English: Y-Yeah... I can see that...

Japanese: （‥‥？　今、真宵ちゃん、 なんか言わなかったか‥‥？）
English: (...? Did Maya say something just now...?)

Japanese: （“やっぱり”‥‥ ‥‥そう聞こえたような‥‥）
English: (I think that she was saying... "I thought so"...)

Japanese: 某月　某日　某時刻 留置所　１３号独房
English: Date and time unknown Detention Center Solitary Confinement Cell 13

Japanese: ‥‥アタクシの‥‥春美。
English: ...My... precious Pearl...

Japanese: ああたこそが、倉院の家元に ふさわしい霊媒師でござあます。
English: You are the only one suitable to be the Master of Kurain, dear child.

Japanese: アタクシは‥‥そのために、 すべてをギセイにした‥‥
English: I sacrificed it all... All for you...

Japanese: あのバカな看護師のために 殺人の計画を立ててやったのも、
English: I helped that brainless nurse carry out her murder,

Japanese: ちゃらちゃらした検事に 協力してやったのも、
English: and cooperated with that whip-happy prosecutor...

Japanese: すべてはあの、 いまいましい本家のムスメ‥‥
English: It was all to unseat that annoying, witless main family girl...

Japanese: 綾里　真宵を ほうむるためだったのに。
English: That Maya Fey.

Japanese: ‥‥待つのです。春美。 チャンスはきっと‥‥
English: ...But I shall be patient, my dearest Pearl... A chance will present itself...

Japanese: ‥‥また、おとずれます‥‥
English: Your time will come...

Japanese: ■第二話　再会、そして逆転 おわり
English: Episode 2: Reunion, and Turnabout THE END

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、綾里　真宵に この場で、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Maya Fey...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!
